head_of_state.title: General Secretary of the CPSU / President of Russia
head_of_state.title_zh: 苏共中央总书记 / 俄罗斯联邦总统

leaders.name: Leonid Brezhnev
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
leaders.background_detail: Trained as metallurgical engineer. Rose through party ranks in Ukraine. Presided over era of stagnation (zastoi) despite initial economic growth.
key_economic_events: Kosygin economic reforms attempted and abandoned
1973 Oil Crisis - windfall for Soviet oil exports
Massive military spending - arms race with US
Growing dependence on oil/gas export revenues
Agricultural failures requiring Western grain imports

leaders.name: Yuri Andropov
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
leaders.background_detail: Former KGB Chairman (1967-1982). Attempted anti-corruption and discipline campaigns. Ill health limited his tenure to 15 months.

leaders.name: Konstantin Chernenko
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
leaders.background_detail: Longtime Brezhnev protégé, party apparatus man. Already seriously ill when appointed. Represented old guard's last stand.

leaders.name: Mikhail Gorbachev
leaders.role: General Secretary of the CPSU / President of the USSR
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
leaders.background_detail: Moscow State University law graduate. Youngest Politburo member. Launched Perestroika (restructuring) and Glasnost (openness). Nobel Peace Prize 1990.
key_economic_events: Oil price collapse (1986) devastated Soviet revenues
Law on Cooperatives (1988) - first private enterprise
500 Days Program debated but not adopted

leaders.name: Boris Yeltsin
leaders.background_detail: Construction engineer turned party official. First democratically elected Russian leader. Oversaw chaotic transition from communism to capitalism.
key_economic_events: Mass privatization via vouchers - rise of oligarchs
1998 Russian Financial Crisis - ruble devaluation and default
Resigned New Year's Eve 1999, appointed Putin acting president

leaders.name: Vladimir Putin
leaders.role: President of the Russian Federation (1st-2nd terms)
leaders.background_detail: Former KGB officer (stationed in Dresden, East Germany). FSB Director before becoming PM then President. Centralized power and restored state authority.
key_economic_events: Economic recovery fueled by high oil prices
National Stabilization Fund created from oil revenues
Rising middle class and consumer spending

leaders.name: Dmitry Medvedev
leaders.background_detail: Law professor and Putin protégé. Served as placeholder in 'tandemocracy' with PM Putin. Focused on modernization rhetoric.
key_economic_events: 2008 Global Financial Crisis hit Russia hard
Russian stock market lost 80% of value
Announced he would not seek re-election, swapped back with Putin

leaders.name: Vladimir Putin
leaders.role: President of the Russian Federation (3rd-5th terms)
leaders.background_detail: Returned to presidency. Constitutional amendments (2020) reset term limits. Escalated confrontation with the West. Re-elected 2024 with reported 87%.
key_economic_events: Annexation of Crimea and Western sanctions (2014)
Invasion of Ukraine (2022-02) - massive Western sanctions
SWIFT disconnection for major Russian banks
Energy war with Europe - gas supply weaponization
Ruble collapse then recovery via capital controls
Pivot to China and India for trade
War economy - military spending dominates budget

central_bank_governor.title: Chairman of Gosbank / Governor of the Central Bank of Russia
central_bank_governor.title_zh: 苏联国家银行行长 / 俄罗斯中央银行行长

leaders.name: Mefody Sveshnikov
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
leaders.background_detail: Soviet banking official. Managed Gosbank during the stable Brezhnev-era planned economy.
key_economic_events: Managed Soviet monetary system during détente era
Oil export revenue management after 1973 crisis
Soviet banking remained subordinate to Gosplan

leaders.name: Vladimir Alkhimov
leaders.name_zh: 弗拉基米尔·阿尔希莫夫
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
leaders.background_detail: Former trade official. Longest-serving Gosbank chairman during late Soviet period.
key_economic_events: Managed Soviet finances during Afghanistan War
Increasing reliance on oil export revenues
Shadow economy expanding beyond state control

leaders.name: Nikolai Garetovsky
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
leaders.background_detail: Brief tenure during early Perestroika period.

leaders.name: Viktor Dementsev
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
leaders.background_detail: Oversaw early banking reforms during Perestroika.

leaders.name: Viktor Gerashchenko
leaders.party_or_affiliation: Communist Party of the Soviet Union
leaders.background_detail: Career Gosbank international banker. Would later serve twice as CBR governor. Called 'the worst central banker in the world' by Jeffrey Sachs.
key_economic_events: Pavlov monetary reform (1991) - 50/100 ruble note swap

leaders.name: Georgy Matyukhin
leaders.background_detail: Academic economist. First head of the new Russian central bank (established as RSFSR state bank before USSR dissolution).
key_economic_events: Establishment of independent Russian central bank

leaders.name: Viktor Gerashchenko
leaders.role: Governor, Central Bank of Russia (1st Russian term)
leaders.background_detail: Returned from Gosbank to lead CBR. Pursued loose monetary policy, enabling massive credit expansion and inflation.
key_economic_events: Black Tuesday (October 1994) - ruble crashed 27% in one day

leaders.name: Tatyana Paramonova
leaders.role: Acting Governor, Central Bank of Russia
leaders.background_detail: First woman to lead CBR. Served as acting governor; Duma refused to confirm her permanently due to political opposition.
key_economic_events: Duma refused to confirm her appointment

leaders.name: Sergei Dubinin
leaders.background_detail: Economist, former Finance Ministry official. Oversaw ruble corridor policy and GKO (government bond) pyramid.
key_economic_events: Ruble corridor exchange rate band policy
GKO market expansion - growing government debt pyramid
1998 Russian Financial Crisis - ruble devaluation and sovereign default

leaders.name: Viktor Gerashchenko
leaders.role: Governor, Central Bank of Russia (2nd Russian term)
leaders.background_detail: Brought back for crisis management after 1998 default. Managed post-crisis stabilization and early recovery.
key_economic_events: Economic recovery as oil prices rose

leaders.name: Sergei Ignatiev
leaders.background_detail: Economist with government service background. Quiet technocrat who served three terms, overseeing Russia's oil boom and 2008 crisis response.
key_economic_events: Oil boom era - massive reserve accumulation
2008 Global Financial Crisis - spent $200B defending ruble
Foreign reserves grew to over $500 billion

leaders.name: Elvira Nabiullina
leaders.background_detail: Economist, former Minister of Economic Development. First woman to head a G8 central bank. Named 'Central Bank Governor of the Year' multiple times by Euromoney. Known for distinctive brooch messaging.
key_economic_events: Shift to inflation targeting and free-floating ruble (2014)
Emergency rate hike to 17% during 2014 ruble crisis
Massive banking sector cleanup - revoked 400+ licenses
Managed sanctions impact from Crimea annexation
2022 Ukraine war emergency measures - rate to 20%
Capital controls to stabilize ruble after SWIFT sanctions
